card_description:
A critical conceptual unit within the top term domain of the Exegesis. Mentions: 326. Referenced 326 times across the corpus. First appears in segments dated 1975-02-27. Bible appears 326 times in the Exegesis corpus. From the Exegesis: "IX From nIXon That’s Latin for 9 IC from dICk That’s Latin for 99

The universe must be read backward; so the three 9s are 666; its’ a – ohhhhhhh. The other night, “Erasmus” laughing. All the codes..." Segment context: "Dick continues writing to Claudia, further elaborating on the spiritual significance of the rat's death." Related to: Acts, Advent, Anamnesis, Because, Christ.

They are going to find that orthogonal axis, Claudia, and when they do, they will find it retrograde and exactly what Joe Chip saw, and it is the time-stream I entered. And the effect of that – it looks like our past. But it is not; it is related to our past, since it moves in that direction. The Bible is a book of the future. The New Testament and the oracles of the Old Testament: they are about tomorrow. The setting, the backdrop, will be

By this I mean to say, Dr. Ornstein, ten months ago this took place, and for ten months I have been a different person. But what to me is most extraordinary (I am writing a book about it, but in the form of fiction, a novel called TO SCARE THE DEAD), is that -- well, let me give the premise as I placed it into the novel:
